title: NOC 72301 – Steamfitters, pipefitters and sprinkler system installers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Steamfitters, Pipefitters, and Sprinkler System Installers (NOC 72301) are the technical powerhouses behind Canada’s industrial growth and public safety. As the country invests heavily in high-density urban housing and advanced manufacturing plants, these professionals are in high demand to install and maintain the complex piping systems that carry high-pressure steam, chemicals, and fire-suppression fluids. Classified as TEER 2, this is a highly skilled trade where safety and precision are paramount. In 2026, the role has evolved to include Smart Fire-Suppression systems and specialized piping for Hydrogen and Carbon-Capture facilities.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Requires college, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group723 – Pipefitting trades

duties: Blueprint Interpretation: Reading and interpreting blueprints, drawings, and specifications to determine the layout of piping and fire-suppression systems.
System Fabrication: Cutting, threading, and bending pipe, and joining sections using various techniques including welding, brazing, soldering, and specialized couplings.
Industrial Installation: Installing high-pressure steam and chemical piping systems in industrial settings such as refineries and power plants.
Fire Safety: Installing and testing automatic fire-sprinkler systems and specialized fire-suppression equipment in residential and commercial buildings.
System Testing: Conducting hydrostatic and air-pressure tests to ensure system integrity and compliance with 2026 Safety and Building Codes.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four- to five-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over five years of work experience and some college/industry courses is required for trade certification.

Certification: Trade certification for steamfitters-pipefitters is compulsory in PEI, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, and Alberta. Certification for sprinkler system installers is compulsory in Quebec and Ontario.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is highly recommended for national mobility and access to high-paying union and industrial roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, particularly in provinces with active industrial and commercial construction sectors:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72301 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was as low as 50, reflecting a high priority for these trades in Ontario’s industrial heartlands.
Alberta: Strong demand. Alberta offers some of the highest wages for this NOC, with a median of $44.90/hr and top earners in the oil sands and heavy industrial plants reaching $56.77/hr.

British Columbia: High Priority. This NOC is currently on the BC PNP Priority Occupations List for construction. Workers with a valid trade certificate and a BC job offer can benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.
National Trend: Over 93% of workers in this NOC receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. The 2026 market favors those with Red Seal endorsement, especially for large-scale infrastructure projects.
Why Work in Canada as a Steamfitter or Sprinkler Fitter?
Strategic Infrastructure Impact: Lead the installation of life-safety systems in new high-rise developments and high-pressure lines in Canada's energy sector.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is high, with a national median wage of $43.89/hr and specialized roles exceeding $110,000 annually.
Cross-Sector Versatility: Work across diverse environments, including construction sites (71%), chemical plants, pulp and paper mills, and nuclear power facilities.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is a staple in BC PNP and OINP draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72301 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with an Ontario job offer.
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): IRCC has updated the trades category for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience (increased from 6 months in 2025).
BC PNP Construction Stream: As a Priority Occupation, NOC 72301 qualifies for targeted BC draws with lower score thresholds for those with a valid BC job offer.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Pipefitters and sprinkler fitters are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
